blocks.content: In a private space known only to himself, Fermi opened the box excavated by the Parrot Mercenary Corps.
Inside were memo papers still covered in dirt and pieces of metal with unknown uses.
It was the future from the past.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: It will take time to restore the memory chip.
It had been buried for a long time, and the hardware itself was severely damaged.
Fermi set the chip aside.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Carefully, carefully.
Complete restoration was difficult, but even a single word could be invaluable.
Using tweezers, Fermi picked up a memo and gently held it under the desk lamp.
Someone's diary.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Let's see.
Or perhaps the last desperate record written before death.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: New Humanity?
Fermi noted the term in his mind and unfolded the twice-folded paper with the tweezers.
It was an official document, half torn as if it had been destroyed, with Eastern characters written on it.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Politicians, always the same…
Even in the face of the world's end, their dedication to not sharing information with other countries was unwavering.
He deciphered the text with flexible thinking.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: The second beast. The number of the beast 666. The three-stage filter reaching the end. The potential rampage of Argones.
Setting the document down, Fermi rested his chin on his hand.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: As expected, the end is unavoidable. Humanity, having reached the singularity, cannot ignore the outside world.
This singularity is divided into three-stage filters.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: The first stage is awareness of the outside world. The second stage is contact with evidence of the outside world. And the third stage is… escaping the photon system.
According to Fermi's analysis, humanity was just passing through the second stage filter.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: The beginning of this is the New Humanity.
More excavation would be needed for detailed information, but it was clear it was related to the outside world.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: When the third stage is reached, the world will end somehow. But "somehow" is a bit…

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: In his thoughts, the end was as difficult as integrating humanity into Ultima.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: It's very hard for any event to reach an extreme. Moreover…

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Hadn't Goffin already escaped the photon system?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Hmm.
Fermi tapped the tweezers on the desk.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Just because one escapes the photon system doesn't mean they enter the outside world.
He wanted to fill the gaps in his reasoning.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Or… the concept of the outside world might be entirely different from what Goffin or we think.
One cannot guard against the unknown.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: A perfect end? Transcending causality? Does this world allow such a system?
Even when Nane tried to close the world, wasn't Shirone there to defend it?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: That's the rule. In a world where extremes exist, there must be corresponding extremes.
Therefore, the end is something that transcends the world.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: It's unimaginable. Beyond the brain's function, it would be something our brains can't process.
What form would it take?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: The end of numbers.
Whatever unimaginable form it takes, it would ultimately come down to numbers.
With a click, the tweezers were fixed on the desk, and Fermi's gaze fell on one of the memos.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: The second beast.
A being born to welcome the first beast and destroy the world.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Where is it?
Shirone would be there.
Holding the official document with the tweezers, Fermi carefully examined the seal on the torn part.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Bukjeon.
Borbor and Tanju Ra, having safely passed through Bukjeon's city gate, entered a brewery.
A scholar with swords at his waist bowed his head.

blocks.speaker: Daehou
blocks.content: You've arrived.
It was Tanju Ra's satellite, Daehou.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Where is Mika?
As the inner tent was drawn back, an old man with a hunched back came out holding a jar.
After confirming the stars, he spoke.

blocks.speaker: The old man
blocks.content: Many of the residents have been replaced by the New Humanity. The owner of this brewery is also one of them.
Borbor's satellite, Mika, was an electromagnetic organism that could parasitize others' bodies.
Having no emotions to begin with, Borbor was indifferent to Mika.

blocks.speaker: Daehou
blocks.content: The mechanism?

blocks.speaker: The old man
blocks.content: They use mental radiation to mutate surrounding organisms. It's similar to the technique used by the Great Star. A tainted mind turns nearby creatures into monsters.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Quite dangerous. It was naive to think we only needed to close the areas where the demonic realm opened.

blocks.speaker: The old man
blocks.content: Mutations are unpredictable. Moreover, this one can control minds. It means he can induce mutations at will.
Tanju Ra's face twisted.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: The ability to induce biological mutations. That's serious. If we don't eliminate it in Bukjeon, the continent will be overturned.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Where is the leader of the New Humanity?

blocks.speaker: Daehou
blocks.content: Mika's ability has searched the city's strongholds, but no one knows the location. It's likely near Governor Hwanggak. Or it could be Hwanggak himself.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: That makes sense. Let's meet the governor and confirm.

blocks.speaker: Mika
blocks.content: What about the body?

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Kill it.
As soon as the order was given, Mika took a knife and stabbed his own heart.
Moments later, the electromagnetic organism emerged from the corpse and merged with Borbor through the current.
- I have taken control of the brainwaves. Shall I think?

blocks.speaker: Mika
blocks.content: No, handle incoming signals. Report any unusual mutations.
- Understood.
The two stars and their two satellites left the brewery and headed straight for the inner gate.
-They are not human.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: What's going on?

blocks.speaker: Mika
blocks.content: We've come to see Governor Hwanggak. If you let us in, we are willing to talk.
As soon as he finished speaking, a powerful aura emanated from the soldiers.
-Stand back. They cannot be analyzed.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Mika sensed a qualitatively different pressure from any organism he had parasitized before.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: If the mutation intensifies at close range… the leader of the New Humanity is in the castle.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Hoho! Non-humans with such authority. What shall we do now?

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Force our way in.
Simultaneously, the soldiers' skin tore, revealing forms that were a mix of human and locust.
Kieeek!
At the monstrous scream from their gaping jaws, Tanju Ra's eyes gleamed with excitement.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Interesting.

blocks.speaker: Daehou
blocks.content: Above!
As Daehou shouted, a swarm of locusts filled the sky from the castle wall.
The sound of chewing was heard.
≪으 으 하 丁?
In front of Governor Hwanggak's corpse, a 17-year-old girl was eating his arm.
She had an innocent look as if she knew no darkness, but her eyes were not human.

blocks.speaker: Piri
blocks.content: Tasty.
As she tore off a piece of arm muscle, the bone was exposed.

blocks.speaker: Piri
blocks.content: Lady Piri.
It wasn't a name given by her parents.
Having evolved into the New Humanity, she gave herself a name meaning 'out of reason.'

blocks.speaker: Piri
blocks.content: Barbelve, is there more of this? Strong human meat. I want to keep eating it.
At the end of Piri's gaze stood a giant locust-human over two meters tall.
Wings hidden under its carapace fluttered, and a scorpion's tail rose above its head.

blocks.speaker: Piri
blocks.content: This is not the time to enjoy eating. There are powerful beings outside the gate.

blocks.speaker: Piri
blocks.content: Really? More tasty meat? What are you waiting for? Go catch them!

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: …Aren't you staying too long? We should destroy this city and leave.

blocks.speaker: Piri
blocks.content: Haha! It's fine. We're strong. You know that, right? No one in this world can beat us.

blocks.speaker: Piri
blocks.content: That was true.
Though they hadn't fought all of humanity, they had a precise understanding of themselves.

blocks.speaker: Piri
blocks.content: Such abilities, such intelligence.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Have we become gods?

blocks.speaker: Piri
blocks.content: Piri extended her hand.

blocks.speaker: Piri
blocks.content: More. Meat.
The locust's eyes watched her, then it spread its wings and turned to the window.

blocks.speaker: Piri
blocks.content: I'll bring it right away.
The locust-humans surrounding Tanju Ra at the inner gate trembled.
Kieeek! Kieeek!
They couldn't move because Tanju Ra had sentenced them.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: For inciting chaos, 600 years in prison! For causing severe mental harm to innocent residents, 987 years in prison!
As soon as the sentence was pronounced, the legal binding force restrained the locust-humans.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Therefore, combining all charges…

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Death.
Simultaneously, Daehou flew in like the wind, swinging his twin swords left and right.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Executioner's Blade.
Like scissors cutting through a landscape, the locusts' heads were sliced off in chunks.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Hoho! Another exemplary punishment today.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Using the leverage law, the Executioner's Blade had theoretically unlimited cutting power.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: That's why he could inflict physical damage even in a 'confined' state.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Don't let your guard down. This alone wouldn't have destroyed the northern gate of the Cheongryong Mountains.
Daehou sheathed his swords.

blocks.speaker: Daehou
blocks.content: These are Bukjeon's mutants. Are they qualitatively different from the Cheongryong Mountains' mutants?
Before he could answer, the castle gate opened.
When they saw a being that didn't look human walking gracefully like a human…
-We must flee.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Mika sent a warning, but Borbor couldn't move a step.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: If I move, I'll die.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: The feeling surrounding the locust-human was so eerie that it gave Borbor that certainty.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Strong? So I can't win? No, it's not that kind of temperament.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: He didn't know.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Even without having seen the entire universe, he felt this being shouldn't exist.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: What's the problem? The aura of its form? The grotesqueness of its evolution? No, these are all things I can imagine.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: The only thing he couldn't imagine was…

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Numbers.
When mathematically expressing the information that made up this being, there was a significant discrepancy.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: It has different numbers from us.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Something that doesn't exist anywhere in the universe.
-I'll handle it.
As Borbor handed over control of his brain to Mika, his body twisted like rubber.
His limbs flew like whips to wrap around the locust-human's body.
-Unable to perceive.
The enemy's form vanished, and a powerful impact hit the back of Borbor's neck.
Ugh!
-89% of brain function is paralyzed. Entering a coma state.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: No. Buy as much time as possible.
-Refusing.
The locust-human, carrying the unconscious Borbor at its waist, looked towards the castle gate.

blocks.speaker: The locust-human
blocks.content: Should I chase them?

blocks.speaker: The locust-human
blocks.content: He hesitated for a moment but then remembered Piri, who would be waiting impatiently.

blocks.speaker: The locust-human
blocks.content: Meat tastes best when eaten alive.
The inner gate closed.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Aaah! Damn creatures!
Though fleeing frantically, his mind was tearing the locust-human apart a thousand times.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Stay focused. Reporting comes first.
As Daehou's ability, Executioner's Blade, split the outer gate, a vast field came into view.

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Huh?

blocks.speaker: Tanju Ra
blocks.content: Great Star?
Shirone was approaching with two apostles.
